pattern: Repeatedly halving the array by alternating min and max pairings reduces a power-of-two sequence to one survivor.
hints: Each round produces a half-length array; you never need to look beyond the current round's pairs.
Pair up adjacent elements; the even output positions keep the smaller, the odd output positions keep the larger.
Loop while the length exceeds one, building the next array from min/max of consecutive pairs, alternating by output index parity.

edge_inputs.description: length eight reduces over three rounds
single element is already the answer
length two takes the min at index zero
the max can win the first round but lose later
all equal values collapse to that value

stress.description: random power-of-two length array exercising many reduction rounds
